hypothesis: A EquiformerV2 model fine-tuned via NN-BAX on a single NEB transition of a given atomic system will, when applied zero-shot to predict NEB paths between other nearby local minima of the same system, yield energy barrier errors below 5% and maximum perpendicular forces within 2× the classical NEB convergence threshold, reducing the number of required simulator evaluations for those new transitions by at least 50% compared to starting from the pre-trained base model.
